title: verbascum incanum
intro: Verbascum incanum, also known as the hoary mullein, is a biennial flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall spikes of white flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of Verbascum incanum have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to support respiratory health and alleviate stress.
